{{Quote|Vengeance is sweet, and I will never rest until Grand Moff Tarkin's blood line has been eradicated from the universe!|Brock Lar|<ref name="daa" />}}
'''Brock Lar''' was once a high-ranking warrior on his homeworld of [[Gryphon]], a planet continually embroiled in clan wars. When [[Brock Lar's youngest son|his youngest son]] died, Lar left Gryphon, realizing that the planet could never attain peace. He made his way to the peaceful planet of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], hoping to eke out a new life. When the Alderaanians heard of his plight, they welcomed him as a citizen, and he found work on the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Martika]]''. Upon returning from a job, however, he found that Alderaan had been destroyed by [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]. Lar declared that he would hunt down [[Tarkin family/Legends|Tarkin's bloodline]], and not rest until they were all eradicated.

==Biography==
Brock Lar was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]], who hailed from the planet [[Gryphon]],<ref name="daa" /> a mountainous world located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="tea">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> At some point, he sired several sons. War among the clans of Gryphon was the regular way of life, and Lar was the highest ranking warrior in his respective clan. When [[Brock Lar's youngest son|his youngest son]] was killed during the wars, however, he realized that peace on Gryphon was impossible. Gathering [[Lar family|his family]], he decided to move away from the certain death that Gryphon held for him, and departed his homeworld, hoping to seek out a more peaceful planet so that his family could start a new life.<ref name="daa" />

[[File:BrockLarBattle.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Brock Lar battling Tusken Raiders.]]
Lar and his family made their way to the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]<ref name="daa" /><ref name="tea" /> where he regaled the natives with his life story. The Alderaanians welcomed Lar and his family, accepting them with open arms. Lar found work as the first mate on the [[Freighter/Legends|cargo ship]] ''[[Martika]]'', and he was able to eke out an entirely new existence. He later embarked on a long shipping job, and returned home to Alderaan, only to find that the planet had been completely [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|obliterated]] by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], merely to demonstrate the battle station's full destructive power. With his family killed in the explosion, Lar reverted to his warring ways, and vowed to hunt down and destroy the [[Tarkin family/Legends|bloodline]] of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], the man who had ordered Alderaan's destruction. He resolved to never sway from his self-adopted task until all of Tarkin's blood relatives had been destroyed.<ref name="daa" /> Although the Grand Moff had perished at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] that had claimed the Death Star,<ref name="anh">{{Film|IV}}</ref> [[Rivoche Tarkin]], Wilhuff's niece, was still alive at the time,<ref name="sf">{{JournalCite|12|Small Favors}}</ref> as was his widow, [[Thalassa Tarkin|Thalassa, Lady Tarkin]].<ref name="plis">"[[Princess Leia, Imperial Servant]]"</ref> At some point, Lar battled [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and held an [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] at knifepoint. He also joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]],<ref name="daa" /> which was waging the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the Empire at the time.<ref name="tea" />

==Personality and traits==
The death of Brock Lar's youngest son made him come to terms with the fact that the clans of Gryphon had no hope for peace. The incident led him to emigrate from his homeworld entirely. Although he was able to find a new career beyond warfare, he reverted to his violent ways when the rest of his family was killed. In Lar's estimation, vengeance was a thing to be savored. Lar had [[Color/Legends|white]] skin, with gray patches, and boasted red eyes.<ref name="daa" />

==Equipment==
[[File:BrockLarKnifey.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Brock Lar brandishing his knife.]]
For attire, Brock Lar wore a green hood, which was part of a torn tunic, the left sleeve of which was all that remained. He wore a gauntlet on his left hand, but his right arm and hand was left bare. Slung across his back was a backpack, as well as a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]]. Lar also kept a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] slung at his hip, the butt of which was attached to a guard on his thigh by a chain. He also boasted a breathing mask, and a [[Knife/Legends|combat knife]], as well as a [[Gaderffii/Legends|gaderffii]].

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Brock Lar was created by [[Matthew Berry]] and [[Craig Farr]], two 18-year-old ''[[Star Wars]]'' fans who entered [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']]{{'s}} "[[Design An Alien]]" competition, which was launched in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]). The competition called on readers to create entirely original aliens inspired and influenced by what was displayed in the [[original trilogy]] of ''Star Wars'' films. While Berry provided the pencils for the final artwork, Farr performed the coloring duties. Although Berry's and Farr's creation was not selected as one of the winners published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] ([[1996]]), they were later published under the "honorable mentions" section in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 8|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 8]]. [[Leland Chee]], the keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]], later confirmed that published material from the "Design an Alien" contest is considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> Although the character of Brock Lar has not been referenced directly in any other ''Star Wars'' material, the planet of Gryphon, which was created in Berry's and Farr's entry, was included in ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ([[2009]]), which was written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]].
